name: mongoimport
description: Import data from a JSON, CSV, or TSV file into a MongoDB instance

options.name: --help
options.description: Returns information on the options and use of mongoimport

name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Increases the amount of internal reporting returned on standard output or in log files

options.name: --quiet
options.description: Runs mongoimport in a quiet mode that attempts to limit the amount of output

options.name: --version
options.description: Returns the mongoimport release number

options.name: --config
options.description: Specifies the full path to a YAML configuration file containing sensitive values for the following options to mongoimport

options.name: --uri
options.description: Specifies the resolvable URI connection string of the MongoDB deployment

name: --host
-h
options.description: Specifies the resolvable hostname of the MongoDB deployment

options.name: --port
options.description: Specifies the TCP port on which the MongoDB instance listens for client connections

options.name: --ssl
options.description: Enables connection to a mongod or mongos that has TLS/SSL support enabled

options.name: --sslCAFile
options.description: Specifies the .pem file that contains the root certificate chain from the Certificate Authority

options.name: --sslPEMKeyFile
options.description: Specifies the .pem file that contains both the TLS/SSL certificate and key

options.name: --sslPEMKeyPassword
options.description: Specifies the password to de-crypt the certificate-key file (i.e. --sslPEMKeyFile)

options.name: --sslCRLFile
options.description: Specifies the .pem file that contains the Certificate Revocation List

options.name: --sslAllowInvalidCertificates
options.description: Bypasses the validation checks for server certificates and allows the use of invalid certificates

options.name: --sslAllowInvalidHostnames
options.description: Disables the validation of the hostnames in TLS/SSL certificates

name: --username
-u
options.description: Specifies a username with which to authenticate to a MongoDB database that uses authentication

name: --password
-p
options.description: Specifies a password with which to authenticate to a MongoDB database that uses authentication

options.name: --awsSessionToken
options.description: Specifies the session token for MONGODB-AWS authentication mechanism

options.name: --authenticationDatabase
options.description: Specifies the authentication database where the specified --username has been created

options.name: --authenticationMechanism
options.description: Specifies the authentication mechanism the mongoimport instance uses to authenticate to the mongod or mongos

options.name: --gssapiServiceName
options.description: Specify the name of the service using GSSAPI/Kerberos. This option is available only in MongoDB Enterprise

options.name: --gssapiHostName
options.description: Specify the hostname of the service using GSSAPI/Kerberos. This option is available only in MongoDB Enterprise

name: --db
-d
options.description: Specifies the name of the database on which to run the mongoimport

name: --collection
-c
options.description: Specifies the name of the collection to import

name: --fields
-f
options.description: Specify a comma separated list of field names when importing CSV or TSV files that do not have field names in the first (i.e. header) line of the file

options.name: --fieldFile
options.description: Specify a file containing a comma separated list of field names when importing CSV or TSV files that do not have field names in the first (i.e. header) line of the file

options.name: --ignoreBlanks
options.description: Ignores empty fields in CSV and TSV exports

options.name: --type
options.description: Specifies the file type to import

options.name: --file
options.description: Specifies the location and name of a file containing the data to import

options.name: --drop
options.description: Modifies the import process so that the target instance drops the collection before importing the data from the input

options.name: --headerline
options.description: Treats the first line of the input file as a header line

options.name: --useArrayIndexFields
options.description: Interpret natural numbers in fields as array indexes when importing CSV or TSV files

options.name: --mode
options.description: Specifies how the import process should handle existing documents in the database that match documents in the import file

options.name: --upsertFields
options.description: Specifies a list of fields for the query portion of the import process

options.name: --stopOnError
options.description: Forces mongoimport to halt the insert operation at the first error rather than continuing the operation despite errors

options.name: jsonArray
options.description: Accepts the import of data expressed with multiple MongoDB documents within a single JSON array

options.name: --legacy
options.description: Indicates that the import data is in Extended JSON v1 format

options.name: --maintainInsertionOrder
options.description: Maintains the order of documents in the import file when inserting documents into the database

options.name: --numInsertionWorkers
options.description: Specifies the number of insertion workers to run concurrently

options.name: --writeConcern
options.description: Specifies the write concern for each write operation that mongoimport performs

options.name: --bypassDocumentValidation
options.description: Enables mongoimport to bypass document validation during the operation

options.name: --columnsHaveTypes
options.description: Instructs mongoimport that the field list specified in --fields, --fieldFile, or --headerline specifies the types of each field

options.name: --parseGrace
options.description: Specifies how mongoimport handles type coercion failures when importing CSV or TSV files with --columnsHaveTypes
